title: ixeridium dentatum subsp. ozense
intro: Ixeridium dentatum subsp. ozense, also known as Ozense Ixeris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), native to Japan. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves which contain sesquiterpene lactones that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In traditional Japanese medicine, the leaves of Ixeridium dentatum subsp. ozense are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat various ailments.
